generation.image_prompt: [TECHNICAL: Aspect Ratio 2:3 Vertical, Edge-to-Edge Continuous, NO TEXT, NO BORDERS] Traditional mystical illustration in the style of 1900s lithography, with clean bold black ink outlines. FULL BLEED: No white margins. The subject is an ANTHROPOMORPHIC Taiwan leopard cat lying on its stomach on a dark road under a stormy sky, wearing tattered mystical robes. It has white spots on the back of its ears and white forehead stripes. EXACTLY 10 silver swords are pierced vertically into its back, arranged in two orderly rows of five. Every hilt must have a corresponding point coming through the other side. Exactly 10 hilts and 10 points. No extra hilts. Dark, tragic, and somber mood.
generation.narrative: The Ten of Swords is 'The Final Toll'—the ultimate tragedy of roadkill, representing the lowest point for a population's survival.

meanings.upright: Tragedy, betrayal, rock bottom, defeat, and painful endings.
meanings.reversed: Recovery, resisting rock bottom, and the possibility of a new beginning after disaster.

ecology.mapping_note: Ten of Swords represents 'Roadkill Mortality'—the most severe threat to leopard cats, serving as a powerful warning of the cost of fragmentation.

website.excerpt: When the road cuts through the heart of the forest, the price is paid in blood. A tragic end, but perhaps a lesson for the survivors.
website.seo_description: Ten of Swords reimagined as the tragedy of roadkill mortality for the Taiwan leopard cat.

meaning.zh: 慘敗、背叛、痛苦的終結，以及路殺與中毒帶來的族群存續危機。
meaning.en: Backstabbing, defeat, a painful ending, and the population crisis caused by roadkill.
